data.title: The Recovery Kit Remix
data.description: A Pelican 1300 cyberdeck inspired by Jay Doscher's iconic Recovery Kit, rebuilt with a Pi 5, a thrifted mechanical keyboard, and a focus on accessibility. Features tool-free component swapping and a modular internal layout.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB)
- **Display**: Waveshare 10.1" IPS touchscreen
- **Keyboard**: Thrifted Redragon K552 mechanical (red switches, compact TKL)
- **Case**: Pelican 1300 (orange — found at a ham radio swap meet for $20)
- **Power**: Baseus 65W USB-C PD 20,000mAh power bank
- **Cooling**: Noctua NF-A4x10 40mm fan
- **Mounting**: Brass standoffs, velcro straps for battery, 3D-printed screen bracket
- **Extras**: USB hub, Ethernet panel mount, power switch with LED indicator

## Total Cost: ~$185 (not counting thrifted parts)

data.title: Fernweh — Solarpunk Bamboo Writing Deck
data.description: An ultra-low-power cyberdeck built into a thrifted bamboo tea box, featuring an e-ink display, BlackBerry keyboard, and solar charging capability. Designed as a distraction-free writing machine for off-grid field work.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
- **Display**: Waveshare 5.65" e-ink (7-color, but used in B/W mode for speed)
- **Keyboard**: BBQ20KBD (BlackBerry Q20 breakout by Solder Party)
- **Case**: Thrifted bamboo tea box ($4 from Goodwill)
- **Power**: PiSugar 2 Pro with 5000mAh battery
- **Solar**: 10W folding USB-C solar panel (Anker)
- **Interior**: Cork shelf liner, brass M2.5 standoffs
- **Decoration**: Wood-burned fern pattern on lid, tung oil finish

## Total Cost: ~$95

data.title: Hex Witch — Dual-Screen Security Research Rig
data.description: A security-focused cyberdeck built into a thrifted aluminum camera case. Features dual displays, a full mechanical keyboard, and a suite of wireless security testing tools. Kali Linux on a Pi 5 with external WiFi adapters and a HackRF SDR.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB) + NVMe HAT with 256GB SSD
- **Primary Display**: 10.1" Elecrow HDMI IPS touchscreen
- **Secondary Display**: 3.5" Pimoroni HyperPixel (system stats / network viz)
- **Keyboard**: Thrifted Keychron K2 wireless mechanical
- **Case**: Aluminum camera case (thrifted from estate sale, $12)
- **Power**: 2x Anker 10,000mAh USB-C PD banks (hot-swappable)
- **Wireless**: Alfa AWUS036ACH (dual-band WiFi), HackRF One SDR
- **Extras**: USB-C hub, panel-mount USB/Ethernet, internal USB fan

## Total Cost: ~$340

data.title: Stardust — Holographic Lunchbox Deck
data.description: A colorful, maximalist cyberdeck built into a thrifted metal lunchbox. Features holographic vinyl wrapping, RGB LED strips, a 7-inch touchscreen, and a custom-painted ortholinear keyboard. Pure queer joy in a portable package.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 5 (4GB)
- **Display**: 7" Waveshare IPS capacitive touchscreen
- **Keyboard**: BM40 ortholinear kit (Gateron milky yellow switches, custom keycaps)
- **Case**: Vintage metal lunchbox (thrifted, $3) + holographic vinyl wrap
- **Power**: Anker 10,000mAh USB-C PD bank
- **Lighting**: WS2812B LED strip (10 LEDs), controlled via GPIO
- **Interior**: Craft foam lining, custom foam-core component trays
- **Extras**: Trans flag stickers, custom boot splash screen

## Total Cost: ~$130

data.title: Aurora Glytch v2 — Acrylic Edge-Lit Cyberdeck
data.description: An upgraded version of the original Aurora Glytch, featuring a fully transparent acrylic case with edge-lit RGB diffusion, built-in audio system with speakers, and a 10-inch capacitive touchscreen. Designed for maximum visual impact.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB)
- **Display**: 10.1" capacitive touchscreen (HDMI + USB)
- **Keyboard**: Magicforce 68-key mechanical (hot-swap sockets)
- **Case**: Custom laser-cut clear acrylic (3 layers, 5mm each)
- **Power**: 2x 5000mAh LiPo cells in parallel + USB-C PD
- **Lighting**: WS2812B LED strips on all edges, digital microphone for audio reactive mode
- **Audio**: 2x 3W class D amplifier + 40mm full-range drivers
- **Extras**: Custom 3D printed stand, acrylic edge polishing kit

## Total Cost: ~$220

data.title: Coral Reef — Bioluminescent Biocompute Deck
data.description: A conceptual deck design featuring bioluminescent materials and living coral-based components. Includes plans for integration with marine biological monitoring equipment and underwater data collection.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
- **Display**: Flexible e-ink 5.83" circular
- **Keyboard**: Silicone roll-up keyboard
- **Case**: 3D printed PLA with bioluminescent filament
- **Power**: Solar-charged supercapacitor + LiPo hybrid
- **Special**: Living coral specimen integration

## Total Cost: ~$75

rejectionReason: Build requires a living coral specimen, which raises significant animal welfare and environmental compliance concerns. This type of biological integration is outside the scope of what we can recommend for home builders. Please revise the concept to use synthetic or non-living materials only.

data.title: Micro Palm-Top Pi6000
data.description: An extremely compact palm-sized cyberdeck built into a vintage calculator case. Features a custom-designed PCB, 3-inch touchscreen, and Choc switches in a 40% layout.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi Compute Module 4
- **Display**: Custom 3" TFT panel with capacitive touch
- **Keyboard**: Custom Choc-switch PCB (40%, integrated)
- **Case**: Repurposed vintage calculator (HP-45)
- **Power**: 1000mAh LiPo + USB-C charging

## Total Cost: ~$180

rejectionReason: The build description includes PCB design files that appear to be copied from another maker's open-source project without attribution. We require original work or proper documentation of derived designs. Additionally, the HP-45 case modification may violate trademark rights for the vintage calculator. Please provide original design work or proper licensing documentation.

data.title: Neon Sign — Budget Cyberdeck From Trash
data.description: A nearly-free cyberdeck built entirely from discarded electronics. Features a broken neon sign transformed into a glowing case, salvaged laptop screen, and a Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W running headless.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi Zero 2W (salvaged)
- **Display**: Inverted laptop screen from e-waste pile
- **Keyboard**: Broken USB keyboard (repairable)
- **Case**: Dead neon sign case (free from curb)
- **Power**: Phone power bank (found)

## Total Cost: ~$0

data.title: Cipher Deck — Encrypted Communication Rig
data.description: A purpose-built encrypted communication system in a cyberdeck form factor. Features two-factor authentication via secure enclave, full-disk encryption, and a custom encrypted mesh networking protocol for off-grid communication.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB)
- **Display**: 7" HDMI touchscreen (privacy filter installed)
- **Keyboard**: Privacy-focused mechanical keyboard with TPM
- **Case**: Faraday cage lined aluminum case
- **Security**: Secure enclave module, hardware key storage
- **Networking**: Mesh networking module (900MHz)

## Total Cost: ~$350

data.title: Prism Prototype v1 — Laser-Cut Acrylic Cyberdeck
data.description: A beginner-friendly cyberdeck design using laser-cut acrylic panels. Designed as a learning project with detailed documentation for others to replicate. Features RGB underglow and a compact layout.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi 4 (4GB)
- **Display**: 7" Waveshare IPS touchscreen
- **Keyboard**: Pre-built 60% mechanical keyboard
- **Case**: Laser-cut acrylic panels (template provided)
- **Power**: 20000mAh USB-C PD power bank
- **Lighting**: WS2812B RGB underglow strip

## Total Cost: ~$150

data.title: BioCode — Biofeedback Monitor Cyberdeck
data.description: A cyberdeck designed for biofeedback monitoring and wellness tracking. Features sensors for heart rate variability, skin conductance, and temperature. Data is displayed on an e-ink dashboard and stored locally in encrypted format.
data.parts_list:
## Parts List

- **SBC**: Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
- **Display**: 4.2" e-ink display (red/black/white)
- **Sensors**: HRV sensor, GSR sensor, contact thermometer
- **Case**: 3D printed bioplastic case
- **Power**: 5000mAh LiPo with solar charging

## Total Cost: ~$120
